rulings.Case Title: TIAN SHAN TRADING (USA), INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. NANCY HUANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 18, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF TIAN SHAN TRADING (USA), INC'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint. Granted. The strong policy in favor of permitting amendments to pleadings, especially when there is no trial date, and the absence of any prejudice to defendants is sufficient good cause to grant the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JANET BRIDGES VS. COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO 'A GOVERMENT ENTITY' et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Amrep, Inc'S Motion To Determine Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 18, 2012, Line 33. Defendant Amrep, Inc'S Motion To Determine Good Faith Settlement. Granted. The City did not satisfy its burden of showing that the AMREP settlement is outside the Tech-Bilt ballpark. The settlement is a fair estimate of AMREP's potential liability given: 1) the order granting Parker-Hannifin motion for summary judgment; 2) the asserted negligence committed by the City; 3) the significant uncertainties in establishing product identification against AMREP; and 4) the significant uncertainties in plaintiff being able to establish her claimed damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE BARBOZA VS. VLADIGOR INVESTMENTS, INC. DBA TOWER CAR WASH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 18, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT VLADIGOR INVESTMENTS, INC. DBA TOWER CAR WASH'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled in its entirety. The second amended complaint is not a sham pleading because the allegations regarding ability to work are not irreconcilable with the allegations in the prior complaint. Plaintiff has adequately alleged that he could perform the job from which he was terminated. None of the claims are brred by the Workers Compensation Act. All of the claims are adequately pled. rulings.Case Title: MK ENTERPRISES INC. VS. JAMES EASON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent James Eason'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 18, 2012, Line 30. Respondent James Eason'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs. Granted as to costs of $450 and denied as to attorney's fees. As the prevailing on the validity of the arbitration award, respondent is entitled to his costs for his first appearance fee and the stipulation (but not this motion. Attorney's fees are only permitted as costs when authorized by contract or statute. The parties' arbitration agreement does not provide that a court can make an award of attorney's fees and respondent does not cite to any statute so providing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PEDRO POLIO, VS. UNITY COURIER SERVICE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Pedro Polio Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And/Or Its Counsel Eric Schreiber In The Amount Of $2,171
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 18, 2012, Line 4. Plaintiff Pedro Polio's Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And/Or Its Counsel Eric Schreiber In The Amount Of $2,171. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has not issued a tentative ruling. Each party should bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 18, 2012, Line 18. DEFENDANT MICHELLE LANDRY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the first, third and fourth causes of action and overruled as to the second cause of action with ten days to answer. rulings.Case Title: PAUL O'GRADY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 18, 2012, Line 23. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's monetary claims are barred by the six months limitations for Government Claims Act claims. Plaintiff's request for injunctive relief is deficient since plaintiff does not allege, or any suggestion that it could allege, that there is any reasonable chance that it will be required to pay the allegedly "unlawful payment" in the future or, even if it would be required to pay the fee again, that a future refund lawsuit is an inadequate remedy. (5 Witkin, California Procedure (5th ed.) "Pleading" sections 823 and 826). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 18, 2012, Line 24. PLAINTIFF KEITH RUCKER DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third affirmative defense, the only issue raised by the demurrer. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
